subject: Added junit for BasicStatusMsg java
commit_message:
Added junit for BasicStatusMsg java

Added junit test case

Issue-ID: AAI-2119
Change-Id: I0f29e295a9e1bd10ccc04a683abdf0f8a35adc99
Signed-off-by: Chandan Ghosh <cghosh12@in.ibm.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-model-loader-master-verify-java/120/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-model-loader-master-verify-java/120

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

This new JUnit test calls only one of the five existing getter methods in BasicStatusMsg.java and therefore increases the code coverage of this class from 18.8% to 31.2%
If we are creating a new test class simply to increase the code coverage, why not add another few lines of code in the same commit in order to reach 100%?
Even better would be to NOT construct this class (which exists purely for implementation purposes) and to instead create a functional test that exercises one or more of the Builder APIs (see CompDoneStatusMessageBuilder.java). This decouples the unit tests from the implementation detail, and reduces some of the maintenance overhead for future developers.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Second attempt to share a link: https://wiki.onap.org/download/attachments/25429038/2018-02-08%20ONAP%20VF2F%20-%20Open%20Source%20Licenses%20and%20Copyright%20Notices.pdf?version=1&modificationDate=1518102785000&api=v2

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

Agreed with Mark.

This test adds no functional value other than to manipulate test coverage figures.

Test coverage numbers are an indicator of lack of functional testing, and not a goal in itself. We should be testing the functional flow that results in the various methods on BasicStatusMsg being invoked, and not simply manually invoking them in the test.

Please update the tests according to the recommendations.
